escalations.reason: Review gate blocked by 2 open contract-violation findings routed back to spec: FINDING-SEC-001 (HIGH, security) -- REQ-005's MUST-NOT ('parser MUST NOT grow its buffer without bound on a stream with the prefix but never a terminator') is FALSE as written: scanOsc's no-terminator branch returns the ENTIRE accumulated buffer, not a small carry-over, so a single 12-byte ORKY_OSC prefix followed by sustained non-terminating PTY output causes unbounded heap growth in the privileged Electron main process (memory-exhaustion DoS) -- the frozen TEST-021 only asserts no-throw/no-emit, never buffer size, so it passes green over the defect. FINDING-CODEX-001 (MEDIUM, contract) -- decodeHeartbeat enforces the 256-byte/charset cap using JS string .length (UTF-16 code units) AFTER splitting into pairs, so an unknown forward-compat pair can carry non-ASCII/control bytes that violate the documented body alphabet while still passing the character-count cap; the documented byte-cap/charset invariant is not actually enforced as specified. Both findings have a concrete code-level fix proposed by the reviewers (add an explicit size ceiling in OrkyOscParser.push's no-terminator carry-over path; validate the whole-body byte length/charset BEFORE splitting into pairs), but escalation_threshold is HIGH and FINDING-SEC-001 is HIGH severity, so per config this needs a human go/no-go rather than an autonomous loopback. RECOMMENDATION: approve loopback to spec (gatekeeper's own route_to) to tighten REQ-005's wording to require an explicit bounded-ceiling guard (not just 'relies on scanOsc carry-over', which is the false premise), then flow through plan/tests/implement to add the fix + a buffer-size-bounded test + a byte-level (not char-length) charset/cap check in decodeHeartbeat before pair-splitting. Both fixes are small and localized to orky-osc-parser.ts; no spec-level scope change needed beyond REQ-005's wording.
escalations.decision: Approved with updated ground truth: Orky has shipped real OSC heartbeat emission (ADR-026) with an authoritative wire contract superseding this feature's placeholder. REAL CONTRACT: introducer ESC ] (0x1b 0x5d), code 9999 (NOT this feature's invented 8888), single ; separator after the code (parser splits on FIRST ; only), payload = single-line compact JSON object (NOT semicolon-delimited key=value pairs), terminator BEL 0x07 (parser MUST accept BEL, MAY additionally accept ST for robustness; Orky only ever sends BEL). Payload schema v1: {v:int always; feature?:string; phase?:string|null; gate?:string N/M of 8 work-phase gates; needsHuman?:boolean; reason?:string only when needsHuman; action?:string|null only when no feature resolvable}. Emitted via gatekeeper CLI 'osc-heartbeat' subcommand, opt-in via config.heartbeat.osc, best-effort/never-throws/writes-nothing. Loop back to SPEC (not just plan/tests/implement) to: (1) replace the OSC contract section with this real documented one verbatim, citing Orky ADR-026, (2) keep FINDING-SEC-001's fix as a binding REQ -- explicit bounded ceiling on the no-terminator carry-over path in the parser, not reliance on scanOsc's unbounded carry-over, (3) keep FINDING-CODEX-001's fix as a binding REQ -- the payload size/charset cap must be validated correctly (byte-accurate, not JS string .length used loosely) for whatever the new JSON-payload validation path looks like. Re-flow plan/tests/implement/review against the corrected contract.

escalations.reason: Review gate blocked by 1 open contract-violation finding (HIGH, escalation_threshold per config): FINDING-DA-004 -- src/shared/orky-status.ts's heartbeatKind() derives kind='done' only when hb.phase==null && hb.gateN>=hb.gateM (matching the FROZEN 02-spec.md REQ-009 acceptance criteria verbatim, which the implementation correctly satisfies). But this was independently verified by the orchestrator directly against the REAL Orky emitter source (C:/dev/Orky/plugin/gatekeeper/gatekeeper.js:904, oscHeartbeat()): phase: state.phase || d.phase || null. Per this repo's own CLAUDE.md gotcha ('Orky status reads active.json.phase + gate topology -- NEVER state.json.phase ... completed features terminate at state.json.phase === doc-sync with human-review recorded only as an external gate'), a genuinely COMPLETE feature's state.json.phase is the truthy string 'doc-sync', never null/unset -- so the real emitter sends {phase:"doc-sync", gate:"8/8", needsHuman:false} for a fully complete feature, NEVER {phase:null}. heartbeatKind's done-branch can therefore never fire against real Orky output: a bare-SSH pane streaming a completed feature's heartbeat renders 'busy' forever, never 'done' -- defeating a core scenario this feature exists for. The defect is in the FROZEN SPEC itself (REQ-009's phase-null-based done-derivation vs. ADR-026/the real emitter's actual state.phase||d.phase||null formula), not the implementation, which correctly implements what REQ-009 says. RECOMMENDATION (not a self-resolution -- reported for human go/no-go per escalation_threshold:HIGH): loopback to spec to correct REQ-009's done-derivation to be gate-based (gateN>=gateM) regardless of phase value -- mirroring 0004's actual gate-based isComplete (f.gates['human-review']?.passed===true) rather than 0004's LOCAL phase-null gateFrontier convention, which is a Termhalla-side computation never carried on the wire -- then re-flow plan/tests/implement/review with a corrected REQ-009 acceptance fixture reflecting the real emitter's phase:'doc-sync',gate:'8/8' shape for a complete feature instead of the current literal-null fixture. Five review lenses (security, quality, devils-advocate, determinism, doc-drift) ran in full; all other findings are MEDIUM/LOW/non-blocking and recorded in findings.json (notably FINDING-SEC-001 and FINDING-CODEX-001, the two findings carried forward from the prior ESC-001 loopback as binding REQ-006/REQ-007, are independently VERIFIED CORRECTLY IMPLEMENTED by the security lens with file:line evidence -- not part of this escalation).

implementer.reason: Router selected A/B mode again (determinism concern), same as the prior cycle. Skipped re-running codex:codex-rescue: this exact feature's immediately prior cycle (recorded in state.json, 2026-06-30T20:29:28) already proved the codex-companion sandbox denies all writes outside the original worktree, so candidate B made zero changes there. Re-running that ~600s experiment under identical conditions (same feature, same worktree) would almost certainly reproduce the identical block, so per orchestrator instruction to treat a non-returning/unavailable codex path as skippable with reason recorded, candidate B was not re-dispatched this cycle. Claude candidate A (orky:implementer) fully implemented the ADR-026 contract (code 9999, JSON payload): build + full test suite green (792/794, the 2 failures are pre-existing environment debris unrelated to this feature, confirmed via git stash reproduction), no frozen test file modified. Winner by default/by merit.

loopbacks.reason: ESC-001 resolved: Orky shipped real OSC heartbeat emission (ADR-026) with an authoritative contract (OSC 9999, JSON payload, BEL-terminated) superseding this feature's 8888/key=value placeholder. Full contract + binding spec requirements written to .orky/features/0014-orky-osc-heartbeat/orky-adr-026-osc-heartbeat.md. Also carry forward FINDING-SEC-001 (unbounded carry-over buffer) and FINDING-CODEX-001 (byte-length cap correctness) as binding REQs in the corrected spec.
